name: Masala Dosa
tagline: Crispy fermented crepe with spiced potato filling

hero.alt: Crisp masala dosa folded over potato filling with coconut chutney and sambar
hero.caption: masala dosa — crisp crepe, potato masala

healthFacts: Fermented dosa batter has a lower glycemic index than plain rice — lactic acid slows glucose absorption.
The fermentation process increases B vitamins, especially B12 and folate.
Urad dal in the batter provides complete protein when combined with rice.
Mustard seeds contain glucosinolates — plant compounds with studied anti-cancer properties.
Curry leaves are rich in carbazole alkaloids that help regulate blood cholesterol.
Naturally gluten-free — a rare comfort food that works for celiac diets without substitutions.

steps.title: Make the aloo filling
steps.detail: Heat oil in a pan. Splutter mustard seeds, then add curry leaves and chana dal. Once the dal turns golden, add sliced onions, green chilies, and ginger. Cook 5 minutes until onions soften.
steps.tip: Curry leaves must go in hot oil to release their aroma. Don't add them to cold oil.

media.alt: Onions, curry leaves, mustard seeds, chana dal, green chilies, and ginger cooking in a pan
media.caption: Tempered onion base

steps.title: Season and mash
steps.detail: Add turmeric and salt to the onion mixture. Stir for 30 seconds. Add the boiled potatoes — roughly mash them directly in the pan, mixing with the onion-spice base. Add coriander. Taste and adjust salt.
steps.tip: Keep some potato texture — completely smooth filling makes the dosa feel soggy inside.

media.alt: Chunky turmeric potato masala with onions, curry leaves, and coriander in a black pan
media.caption: Chunky potato masala

steps.title: Heat the tawa
steps.detail: Heat the tawa on medium-high. Sprinkle a few drops of water — it should evaporate in under a second. Drizzle a few drops of oil and wipe with a half-onion or folded cloth. The surface should look lightly shiny.
steps.tip: The onion wipe seasons the tawa and prevents sticking. This is the traditional method.

media.alt: Hot black tawa with water droplets steaming on an oil-wiped surface
media.caption: Hot seasoned tawa

steps.title: Pour and spread the batter
steps.detail: Pour a ladleful of batter in the center. Immediately start spreading in concentric outward circles with the back of the ladle, applying light even pressure. Work fast — the batter sets quickly. Aim for a thin, even round.
steps.tip: Speed matters. The batter should be spread within 5–8 seconds of hitting the pan.

media.alt: Pale dosa batter spread in thin concentric circles on a hot black tawa
media.caption: Thin batter spiral

steps.title: Crisp the dosa
steps.detail: Drizzle ½ tsp oil around the edges and a few drops in the center. Cook on medium-high until the edges lift naturally and turn golden-brown. The top surface should look dry and matte — no wet patches. This takes 2–3 minutes.
steps.tip: Resist the urge to flip. Dosa is almost always cooked on one side only for the crisp.

media.alt: Golden dosa crisping on a tawa with dry lacy surface and browned edges
media.caption: Crisp golden dosa

steps.title: Fill and fold
steps.detail: Place 2–3 tbsp of potato filling on one half of the dosa. Fold the other half over it. Or roll it — place filling along the center and roll into a cone or cylinder. Serve immediately with coconut chutney and sambar.
steps.tip: Dosa goes soft within minutes. Serve straight from the tawa.

media.alt: Crisp dosa folded around yellow potato masala on a black tawa
media.caption: Filled and folded

utensils.name: Cast iron or non-stick tawa (flat griddle)
Small bowl of water (for sprinkling tawa)
